steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The first sentence establishes that poisonous amphibians migrate during the day. The second sentence explains how they do so safely: their warning colors deter daytime predators. The transition must connect the time setting (daytime) introduced in the first sentence to the protective mechanism in the second.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'At that time,': 'At that time' refers back to the daytime period just established and introduces what happens then — the warning colors provide protection. This is the right temporal link. ✓
  • Choice B — 'For example,': 'For example' signals that the second sentence illustrates something from the first. The second sentence is not an example; it is an explanation of how daytime migration works. ✗
  • Choice C — 'In other words,': 'In other words' would mean the second sentence restates the first. The second sentence adds new information about warning colors, so it is not a restatement. ✗
  • Choice D — 'However,': 'However' signals contrast. The second sentence does not contradict the first — it elaborates on it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
